question_text: A planet orbits the Sun with an orbital radius of 10 AU (Astronomical Units). If Earth has an orbital radius of 1 AU and an orbital period of 1 year, approximately how many years does this planet take to orbit the Sun?
correct_answer: Approximately 32 years (using Kepler's Third Law: T² is proportional to r³, so T² = 10³ = 1000, therefore T = √1000 ≈ 31.6 ≈ 32 years)
explanation: This uses Kepler's Third Law, which states that the square of the orbital period is proportional to the cube of the orbital radius. By cubing the distance ratio (10³) and taking the square root, students find the period ratio.
